feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Cardiologists Reveal 3 Things That Are Quietly Impacting Your Cholesterol
entry.summary: The cholesterol culprits no one talks about (but you should know about).

entry.title: Simple diet change could relieve painful bathroom symptoms, experts say
entry.summary: New AGA guidelines emphasize lifestyle changes like increased fiber intake and less toilet time over creams and quick fixes for hemorrhoid relief.

entry.title: Popular Vitamin D Supplement Has “Previously Unknown” Negative Effect, Study Finds
entry.summary: A new meta-analysis is challenging long-standing assumptions about vitamin D supplements. New research from researchers at the University of Surrey, the John Innes Centre, and Quadram Institute Bioscience is raising questions about a common choice in suppleme…

entry.title: Experts Say People Who Stay Fit Into Their 60s and 70s Aren't the Ones With the Best Genetics or Strictest Routines, They're the Ones Who See Movement Differently
entry.summary: Some older adults keep moving for decades, but the reason may be less obvious than discipline, genes or a perfect routine.

entry.title: Column | America’s obesity problem isn’t about costs. We just love junk food.
entry.summary: Junk food is cheaper than healthful food, but a new study reinforces taste preferences play a bigger role than prices.

entry.title: More people are heading to the ER for tick bites this year. Here’s why.
entry.summary: Experts point to several reasons for the larger-than-usual spike, but there are easy ways to protect yourself.

entry.title: Exercising at This Time of Day May Lower Your Blood Pressure by Nearly Twice as Much
entry.summary: Research says this matters almost as much as whether you work out at all, especially if you’re already at risk for heart disease.

entry.title: Does drinking water prevent kidney stones? Here’s what experts say.
entry.summary: Increased hydration is considered the best way to prevent kidney stones. A new study suggests it’s not that easy.

entry.title: Magic mushrooms could help people quit smoking
entry.summary: Nicotine is highly addictive, but new research is showing that psychedelics can shift people's worldview in ways to help them give up cigarettes.

entry.title: Why stomach pain, ‘scromiting,’ and compulsive hot showering is sending cannabis users to Philly-area ERs
entry.summary: Marijuana legalization and more potent THC products could be to blame for the rise in cannabinoid hyperemesis syndrome, doctors say.

entry.title: New treatment cuts bad cholesterol by nearly 50% without statins
entry.summary: A new breakthrough could change how high cholesterol is treated, offering a powerful alternative to traditional drugs. Researchers have developed tiny DNA-based molecules that shut down PCSK9—a key protein that keeps “bad” LDL cholesterol circulating in the b…

entry.title: AI Can Spot Pancreatic Cancer Years Before Diagnosis, Study Finds
entry.summary: Pancreatic cancer is on course to be the second-leading cause of cancer-related death in the US by 2030, in part because 85 percent of cases aren't diagnosed until the disease has spread.

entry.title: Scientists identify exactly how and why coffee fights aging and boosts overall health
entry.summary: Scientists found that NR4A1 compounds in coffee interact with a cell receptor that helps reduce stress and inflammation.

entry.title: RFK Jr.’s Unsupported Claims About Tylenol-Autism Study He Called ‘Garbage’
entry.summary: During an April 17 congressional hearing, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. called for retraction of a new Danish study that didn't find a link between Tylenol and autism, repeatedly calling it “garbage” and baselessly suggesting that …

entry.title: Love the skin you’re in? Check it for signs of cancer
entry.summary: Melanoma Monday, which this year falls on May 4, is an annual day aimed at spreading awareness about skin cancer

entry.title: Childhood trauma linked to biological aging and gaze avoidance
entry.summary: Childhood abuse forces the biological clock to tick faster and curbs children's willingness to make eye contact, independent consequences that both track with higher rates of emotional and behavioral problems.

entry.title: The Men Who Want to Supersize Their Testicles
entry.summary: Men explain the erotic appeal of using saline inflation to increase the size of their balls, despite serious risks to their sexual health.

entry.title: Genetic association and machine learning improve the prediction of type 1 diabetes risk
entry.summary: Genome-wide association and fine-mapping analyses of type 1 diabetes (T1D) identify multiple genetic risk signals. Furthermore, a machine learning model, T1GRS, improves the prediction of T1D in individuals with complex risk profiles and identifies genetic su…

entry.title: Mercy Health launches new trial to reduce chemo nerve damage
entry.summary: A new clinical trial in the Springfield area is giving cancer patients access to a potential way to reduce a common and often painful side […]

entry.title: Glucose Levels Signal the Growth of Myelin
entry.summary: New research reveals that high glucose levels drive stem cells to multiply, while low levels signal them to mature into myelin-producing cells, a discovery that could lead to new metabolic strategies for treating white matter injuries and MS.

entry.title: 6 Daily Exercises That Firm Waist Thickening Faster Than Weight Lifting After 50
entry.summary: Certified trainers share 6 daily moves that target waist thickening after 50, including planks, dead bugs, and cardio.

entry.title: Extensive review finds only modest relief and major risks from a common go-to pain drug
entry.summary: A large review of existing research took a hard look at how well the pain pill tramadol actually works for chronic pain.

entry.title: The invisible force making food less nutritious
entry.summary: Surging concentrations of carbon in the atmosphere have produced potent changes in the way plants grow, draining the nutrients from food.

entry.title: Misidentified 'edible' mushrooms linked to a surge of ALS cases
entry.summary: A recent study discovered measurable poison in wild false morel mushrooms eaten before a rare cluster of the fatal nerve disease ALS.

entry.title: 4 Standing Exercises That Build Thigh Strength Faster Than Leg Press After 60
entry.summary: A certified personal trainer shares 4 standing exercises that rebuild thigh strength and improve balance and coordination after 60.

entry.title: Scientists Say This Simple Habit May Help Lower Blood Pressure Naturally
entry.summary: Can drinking water lower blood pressure? Cardiologists explain the link between hydration and heart health, plus other ways to lower BP naturally.

entry.title: Whooping cough outbreak: Pasadena health officials confirm 4 cases at Don Benito Fundamental School
entry.summary: Pasadena health officials are investigating a whooping cough outbreak after four cases were confirmed at a school.

entry.title: Summer 2026 Will Be Bad for Mosquitoes—Here’s How to Keep Them Away from Your Outdoor Space
entry.summary: Buckle up, it's about to get buggy. Erratic winter weather, surging spring temperatures, and high precipitation all mean a bad mosquito season for 2026.

entry.title: Serious mental illness is often connected to homelessness. It doesn’t have to be, Portland professor says
entry.summary: Shannon Blajeski explains how people with serious mental illness can live independently and not be stuck in poverty.

entry.title: Dear Doctor: Repatha kept my cholesterol low without muscle aches I got on statins. Does it work as well?
entry.summary: Repatha, a PCSK9 inhibitor, lowers cholesterol well, especially for those intolerant to statins, but its absolute risk benefit is small.

entry.title: Dietitians Reveal the 10 Drinks to Never Drink for Better Health
entry.summary: Nutrition experts share the drinks to never drink if you're trying to be proactive about your health. Plus, the better-for-you alternatives to try instead.

entry.title: The World’s Rarest Blood Type Just Got Even Stranger, Only 3 People on Earth Have It
entry.summary: Scientists discover new blood type that doesn’t fit any known category, and standard tests can miss it.

entry.title: The harder you work for something, the better the dopamine response
entry.summary: When it comes to dopamine, we act like kids in a candy store. But instead of overindulging, experts suggest looking for sources that take a little more work.

entry.title: 'Two lives hang in the balance': Risky surgery in the womb saved baby from deadly disorder at just 25 weeks gestation
entry.summary: To save a baby with a rare lung disorder, doctors performed a surgery while he remained half-in and half-out of the womb.

entry.title: Dietitians Warn Against Eating One Food Before Bed for Better Sleep
entry.summary: Honestly, we're a bit salty about this one.

entry.title: Why beets are good for your heart and how to get more of them in your diet
entry.summary: Scientists have found that beets and other nitrate-rich vegetables may help lower blood pressure.

entry.title: Brain scans reveal 3 ADHD subtypes, including a more extreme form
entry.summary: A study analyzing 1,154 children and adolescents breaks new ground in how to think about the growing diagnosis.

entry.title: To catch colorectal cancer early, advocates push to make ‘poop talk’ OK
entry.summary: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.

entry.title: ‘Game changer’: New tech is reducing Parkinson’s symptoms in South Florida patients
entry.summary: South Florida patients report Parkinson’s disease symptom relief from DBS, HIFU and other technology innovations at Memorial, Baptist and University of Miami.

entry.title: Scientists restore memory by blocking a single Alzheimer’s protein
entry.summary: Researchers have identified a new potential weapon against Alzheimer’s: blocking a protein called PTP1B. In mice, this approach boosted memory and helped brain immune cells clear harmful plaque buildup. Since PTP1B is also linked to diabetes and obesity—both …

entry.title: A 29-year-old man was told his constipation was nothing to worry about. He was diagnosed with colon cancer.
entry.summary: Young cancer cases are rising but a significant number are diagnosed late. A man was given laxatives twice before doctors discovered his colon cancer.

entry.title: Why the Maine Atlas and Gazetteer is still a page-turner
entry.summary: Mainers continue to mark up routes and dog-ear pages 50 years after it was first published, despite the ease and abundance of digital navigation.

entry.title: Scientists reveal the best exercise for knee arthritis pain relief
entry.summary: A major review of 217 trials shows that aerobic exercise is the most effective option for managing knee osteoarthritis. Activities like walking, cycling, and swimming outperformed other exercise types in reducing pain and improving movement. While alternative…

entry.title: Brief daily lying-down exercise linked to better balance and agility
entry.summary: A Japanese study suggests that a daily exercise routine people can perform on the floor may enhance balance and mobility, and could have use for older adults or those in rehabilitation settings.

entry.title: Sleepy’ Golden Retriever Doesn’t Want To Let Go of His Toy
entry.summary: This Golden Retriever won’t let go of his toy despite being sleepy, and the adorable struggle is winning hearts online.

entry.title: Psychology says people who quietly leave parties early without saying goodbye to everyone aren't being rude — they've simply learned that their presence was never as central to the room as their anxiety told them it was, and that realization is both humbling
entry.summary: This revelation transforms every social gathering from an exhausting performance into a choice, and understanding why your quiet exit goes unnoticed might be the most freeing realization you'll ever have about human connection.

entry.title: Dorothy Benner Martin
entry.summary: It is with profound sadness that we announce the passing of Dorothy Benner Martin, who departed this life peacefully on April 27, 2026, at the age of 92. Born on

entry.title: Scientists Reveal Why Breast Cancer Risk Rises With Age
entry.summary: A woman's breast tissue undergoes dramatic changes as it ages, creating an environment where cancer cells are more likely to flourish, a new study has found.

entry.title: Magnesium may help people sleep better, but the new study comes with a warning
entry.summary: A study shows that magnesium improves sleep, but experts warn of limitations and risks associated with its use as a supplement

entry.title: Endometriosis: New scan technique shows promise for earlier detection
entry.summary: A new scan technique could spot areas of endometriosis missed by conventional scans, scientists say.
